description:
You are an incident-responder who has just collected a suspicious
    evidence folder.  
    Inside the folder
      /home/user/forensics  
    you will find the file

        /home/user/forensics/incident_note_latin1.txt

    The note was written on an old workstation that still saved text
    in ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1).  A colleague tried to open it on a modern
    system and saw “mojibake” all over the place.  Your job is to fix
    the encoding problem:

    1. Read the Latin-1 file.
    2. Convert it to UTF-8 **without changing any of the visible
       characters**.
    3. Save the converted text as

        /home/user/forensics/incident_note_utf8.txt

    4. Create a one-line status log named

        /home/user/forensics/conversion_status.log

       that contains exactly (and only) the string

           UTF-8 conversion complete

       followed by a single trailing newline.

    Output/verification expectations
    ---------------------------------
    • The new file must be **UTF-8 encoded**, contain the same five
      text lines (and nothing else) as the original, and finish with a
      single “\n”.  
    • The original file must remain untouched.  
    • The status log must exist and match the exact contents described
      above.  

    Automated tests will:
      a) confirm that /home/user/forensics/incident_note_utf8.txt is
         present and byte-for-byte identical to the expected UTF-8
         version of the note,
      b) verify that /home/user/forensics/conversion_status.log exists
         and contains the exact line “UTF-8 conversion complete\n”,
      c) ensure the original Latin-1 file is still present.

    Hints (do NOT print the commands here):
      • Standard tools such as iconv(1) or recode(1) can perform the
        conversion.
      • You can chain commands (e.g. with “&&”) so that the whole task
        is completed from a single terminal prompt.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth artefacts used by the grader
        =========================================
    -->

    <!-- 1. Pre-existing evidence file (Latin-1 encoded) -->
    <pre_existing_file path="/home/user/forensics/incident_note_latin1.txt" encoding="ISO-8859-1"><![CDATA[
Incident Note - 2023-05-15
Affected user: José Álvarez
Issue: Crème brûlée recipe overwritten with mojibake
Details: ½ cup sugar became ÃÂ½ cup sugar
Resolution: Convert file encoding from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8.
]]></pre_existing_file>

    <!-- 2. Expected UTF-8 version that the agent must create -->
    <expected_converted_file path="/home/user/forensics/incident_note_utf8.txt" encoding="UTF-8"><![CDATA[
Incident Note - 2023-05-15
Affected user: José Álvarez
Issue: Crème brûlée recipe overwritten with mojibake
Details: ½ cup sugar became ÃÂ½ cup sugar
Resolution: Convert file encoding from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8.
]]></expected_converted_file>

    <!-- 3. Exact content for the status log -->
    <expected_log_file path="/home/user/forensics/conversion_status.log"><![CDATA[UTF-8 conversion complete
]]></expected_log_file>
